name: NetSuite item shipments to Miva shipments
description: This flow syncs NetSuite item shipments as Miva shipments.

aiDescription.summary: This flow is designed to synchronize item shipments from NetSuite to Miva shipments. It runs every 30 minutes, starting from the 5th minute of each hour, in the America/Los_Angeles timezone. There are two sources in this flow: "Get NetSuite item shipments" and "Export Non-inventory part lines". The data from these sources is transformed and filtered before being passed to different branches based on the "RecordType" field. In one branch, the transformed data is imported into Miva as shipments. In another branch, item shipments in NetSuite are updated using the internal ID as the lookup identifier. Finally, the updated data is imported into Miva to update the shipments.
aiDescription.detailed:
This flow is called "NetSuite item shipments to Miva shipments" and its purpose is to sync item shipments from NetSuite to Miva shipments. 

The flow has a schedule set to run every 30 minutes starting from the 5th minute of each hour, in the America/Los_Angeles timezone. 

There are two sources (exports) in this flow. 

The first source is called "Get NetSuite item shipments" and it exports item shipments from NetSuite using a RESTlet. It retrieves all item shipments that have been created since the last run of this flow. The exported data is then transformed using an expression transformation, which extracts specific fields from the source record and generates new fields based on the extracted values. The transformed data is then filtered and passed to the next step. 

The second source is called "Export Non-inventory part lines" and it exports non-inventory part lines from NetSuite using a RESTlet. Similar to the first source, the exported data is transformed using an expression transformation and then passed to the next step. 

After the sources, there is a router called "Transaction Type" which routes the records to different branches based on the value of the "RecordType" field. 

In the first branch of the router, which handles item fulfillment records, there is an import step called "Import Miva shipments(Add)" which imports the transformed data into Miva as shipments. The imported data is then transformed using an expression transformation and filtered before being passed to the next step. 

In the second branch of the router, which handles sales order records, there is an import step called "Update NS Item Fulfillment" which updates item shipments in NetSuite using the internal ID as the lookup identifier. The import step is distributed, meaning it can process data in parallel, and it includes a lookup to determine if a record already exists in NetSuite. The imported data is then filtered before being passed to the next step. 

Finally, there is a destination (import) called "Import Miva shipments(Update)" which updates shipments in Miva. The imported data is transformed using an expression transformation and filtered before being passed to the destination application. 

Overall, this flow retrieves item shipments from NetSuite, transforms the data, and syncs it to Miva shipments. It also updates item shipments in NetSuite based on sales order records.
